reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 6 gates passed. Gate 3 verified: ComputeForkDigest (signing_root.go:304-310) only uses fork_version and genesis_validators_root, ignoring blob parameters entirely. The correct implementation exists in config.go:570-590 (entryWithForkDigest) which XORs hash(epoch||max_blobs) for Fulu epochs, but bootnode.go:221 bypasses this by calling signing.ComputeForkDigest directly. Gate 4 confirmed attacker control: an attacker can configure a bootnode with same ForkVersion but different MaxBlobsPerBlock for Fulu, producing identical fork digests and causing network partition when victim nodes connect. Spec reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-027 'Fork digest must differ when blob parameters change, even if fork version is unchanged (EIP-7892)'. Severity: Prysm's 31% network share caps impact, and bootnode usage varies, so realistic impact is likely <5-10% of network (Medium per severity_classification), though this is a trivial network partition attack. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by running a malicious bootnode with Fulu fork version and different blob parameters, causing consensus failures and chain splits for connecting nodes.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Fork digest must differ when blob parameters change, even if fork version is unchanged (EIP-7892).
